title: calea bucaramangensis
intro: Calea bucaramangensis, also known as 'Santa Cruz de Juazeiro', is a plant species native to Colombia and Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which have been associated with its ability to soothe digestive issues and relieve pain. The health benefits of Calea bucaramangensis include its potential to reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. Calea bucaramangensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
